steps: Find the slope using two table points: \(m = \frac{18 - 8}{6 - 3} = \frac{10}{3}\).
Find the \(y\)-intercept using point \((3, 8)\): \(8 = \frac{10}{3}(3) + b \Rightarrow 8 = 10 + b \Rightarrow b = -2\).
The equation is \(P = \frac{10}{3}s - 2\). Verify with \(s = 9\): \(P = 30 - 2 = 28\). Correct.

choices.explanation:
• Test \(s = 3\): \(P = 9 + 10 = 19\). The table says 8.00.
  • The slope is 3, not \(\frac{10}{3}\), so this does not match the rate of change in the table.
  • Eliminated because it fails the first data point.

• Test \(s = 3\): \(P = \frac{10}{3}(3) + 8 = 10 + 8 = 18\). The table says 8.00.
  • The slope is right but the intercept is wrong; adding 8 instead of –2 shifts the line up by 10.
  • Eliminated because it fails the first data point.

• Test \(s = 3\): \(P = 10 - 2 = 8\). Matches. Test \(s = 6\): \(P = 20 - 2 = 18\). Matches. Test \(s = 9\): \(P = 30 - 2 = 28\). Matches.
  • Slope \(\frac{10}{3}\) and intercept \(-2\) were derived directly from the table data.
  • That matches choice C — C is correct.

• Test \(s = 3\): \(P = 0.9 - 0.1 = 0.8\). The table says 8.00.
  • This slope \(\frac{3}{10}\) is the reciprocal of the correct slope and is far too small.
  • Eliminated because all three table values are off by an order of magnitude.
